Hey Product Hunt 👋 I'm Clément, founder of Rerun. We built Rerun because every "AI agent" tool felt like a black box. You fire it off and just hope it did the right thing. We wanted agents you could actually watch work, step by step, that pause before doing anything sensitive. Rerun lets you build no-code AI agents that run 24/7 (chasing invoices, qualifying leads, clearing your inbox), connected to the tools you already use. Live dashboards show every run, token, and decision. Each workspace gets its own private server, no shared tenancy. We'll be here all day. We'd genuinely love your feedback, questions, and ideas on what to automate next. [...] Subscribe

results.title: Best No-Code AI Agent Builders in 2026: Ranked & Tested | Rerun
results.content:
Rerun is the best option for small businesses that need production-grade agents with full visibility into what those agents are doing. Lindy is a strong alternative for teams focused on sales and support who want faster initial setup. For simple single-step AI automations, MindStudio offers a free tier with no technical barrier to entry.

Yes. Rerun offers a free 3-hour trial with no credit card required — long enough to build, test, and run a real production agent from scratch. Lindy also has a free tier that includes a meaningful number of task credits per month. Both let you connect integrations and run agents without entering payment information upfront. [...] A no-code AI agent builder with built-in observability is not just a simpler way to build agents. It is the difference between deploying an agent and actually running one.

Rerun — connect 110 plus tools and build agents that take real action across your stack

## Frequently asked questions

Rerun offers a free 3-hour trial with no credit card required, giving you enough time to build and test a real agent. Lindy has a free tier with meaningful task credits for simple automations. Relevance AI's free tier is capped at 200 actions per month, which is tight for meaningful evaluation. For teams that want genuine no-code production capability, Rerun's trial is the most complete free starting point. [...] Switching from Relay.app? Relay.app shut down in 2025, leaving thousands of teams looking for a replacement that goes beyond basic automation. Rerun is the direct migration path: your automations become autonomous agents with native run monitoring, deployable in an afternoon. Start your free migration →

Rerun — build autonomous AI agents and watch them work live on your dashboard

## #1 Rerun: The Only No-Code Builder Where You Watch the Work Happen

Rerun is purpose-built for agent orchestration. It was not adapted from workflow automation or chatbot infrastructure. Build, deploy, and monitor are a single integrated product rather than three separate problems stitched together.

results.title: What is currently the best no-code AI Agent builder?
results.content:
## Top Comments
- I've tried WorkfxAI, which has companion agent help to build AI Agent, quite easy to use.
- Give Tars a try, easy to use and accurate responses and tool calling.
- QuickAgent - truly easiest point-and-click way to build AI agents that connect to your business tools
- Chitchatbot.ai can deploy on website and socials. Integrates with all popular llms. Starts from just $15 per month, you can just delete contacts to stay within this tier. Very powerful platform. [...] to an agent, load the agent with Client A’s brand voice and audience data, then let it handle all content requests for that client. The core copywriting logic stays the same; only the context changes. When Client B shows up, you do not clone a jungle of workflows and copy paste a whole set of stray variables. You simply spin up a new agent, assign the same tool, point it at Client B’s data, and press go. Relevance Ai was early to enable creating agents with just plain prompt. Zapier is obviously the incumbent in this space quickly catching up and likely with the resources and support if that’s the priority. Langchain is the among the top and the most used framework- but heavy on the dev side of things catering at the code level. While the OAP and Langflow are approachable no-code tools [...] (all that annoying n8n screenshots that fill the LinkedIn and YouTube feed). With agents, you equip an agent with set of tools that the agent can call and perform actions based on the task/request, making tool calling decisions on the fly, so every agentic run is unique and task oriented. In essence, this means you can modularize your core set of tasks as workflows/tools. And then create a few domain specific agents and assign tools to them. This allows for a far more efficient and scalable system for productivity. For example: Instead of wiring a separate flow for every task like blog posts, social updates, email campaigns, social listening, research. You just treat “marketing copywriting” as one reusable tool. Give that tool to an agent, load the agent with Client A’s brand voice and

results.title: We Tried and Tested 8 Best AutoGen Alternatives to Build AI Agents and Applications - ZenML Blog
results.content:
Orchestrate multi-step workflows on a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) model that lets you create event-driven workflows with explicit steps.
 Use built-in modules like the `FunctionAgent` and `AgentWorkflow` to orchestrate multi-step tasks that involve using tools or querying data in a structured way.
 Built-in advanced indexing and retrieval tools that help you build agents that reason over your documents, databases, or APIs.
 Handle hundreds of data sources and millions of tokens with features like automatic text chunking, embedding caching, and incremental index updates.
 Agents in a LlamaIndex workflow communicate indirectly through a shared `Context` object. This provides a structured way to pass state and information between agents without relying on parsing conversational history. [...] ### Features

 Offers multi-agent orchestration as a first-class feature, you can build agents with tools, knowledge, and shared state, and organize them as teams so they can collaborate on tasks.
 Supports multi-modal by default, so agents can handle text, images, audio, and video inputs/outputs.
 Built-in SQL and data tools enable agents to query databases, analyze datasets, and generate visualizations without custom tool implementation.
 Built with production in mind, offers easy integrations with web frameworks like FastAPI and tools for logging and monitoring.

### Pricing

Agno is completely free to get started (open source). It offers two paid plans as well:

 Starter: $150 per month
 Custom: Custom pricing [...] 📚 Also read: CrewAI alternatives

### Features

 Use different collaboration patterns like sequential, hierarchical, and turn-based to execute tasks in a structured and deterministic manner.
 Built-in memory systems allow agents to recall past interactions and build knowledge over time without manual intervention.
 Record and reply agents run to inspect each step and even modify a step’s outcome, then resume execution from that point to test fixes.
 Equip agents with pre-built tools, like web search, code execution, web scraping, or add custom tools using Python functions.
 Integrate with third-party observability and ML monitoring tools, like Langfuse, Arize Phoenix, MLflow, and more.

### Pricing
